change_summary: Change the material to marble while maintaining the same dimensions

cascade_issues.title: Thermal Gradient Exceeds Limit
cascade_issues.description: Marble has a higher thermal conductivity than typical materials used in spacecraft, which could increase the thermal gradient across the optical bench beyond the 15°C limit.

cascade_issues.title: Shock Test Required
cascade_issues.description: Marble is brittle and may not withstand the shock loads during launch, necessitating a new shock test to validate its structural integrity.

cascade_issues.title: Mass Increase
cascade_issues.description: Marble is denser than typical spacecraft materials, leading to an increase in the total mass of the payload. This could violate the 35 kg mass budget.

cascade_issues.title: Solar Panel Mounting Issues
cascade_issues.description: Marble may not provide adequate mounting points or structural support for the solar panels, potentially affecting their deployment and alignment.

cascade_issues.title: Stray Light Concerns
cascade_issues.description: Marble's reflective properties could introduce stray light into the star tracker, affecting its performance and accuracy.

ecss_open_validation_items.activity: Thermal balance test to validate TMM
Thermal vacuum test at qualification margins (4 cycles min)
Shock test per launcher SRS ICD
